title: Reis Appartment
wikitext:
[]: Rei's Appartment             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jul 06, 2011 1:01 pm When we first see it in 1.X, it looks just like it did in the TV Series. But if you look at the scenes where it appears in 2.X, you'll notice that the buildings around it - and parts of the building it's in are being knocked down. In old!NGE, the row of houses next to it was already knocked down when we first saw it (like in 1.X) but despite all the construction noise we kept hearing, we never *saw* any actual house demolishing taking place (a common misconception is that Rei is basically out there with the rats, the delinquents and the thungs who could rape her at any time - But the place isn't a council estate she was put in out of neglect or for simplicity - in fact, the place is completely abandoned. It's empty, many door are missing or standing open . Also, ep. 23 sort of implies that she lives there because she likes it/ she's somehow trying to "replicate" her old room.) Now, in Rebuild, we see the excavators and cranes, and the demolished rubble that is. The part of the apparment Rei is living in remains alone in a field of debris. Y'guys think there's any meaning in it? I have a few possible theories: a) Anno's correcting an oversignt. Perhaps it wasn't obvious enough that the place is abandoned/the knocking-down of buildings should technically advance. Or maybe he thought it looks more desolate this way. b) It's meant to reinforce the whole "change" motif. Old stuff gets demolished so new stuff can be made. c) Maybe it's meant to showcase Rei's developement into individuality - At first, she was one of many clones that were as similar to each other as the various buildings. As the story progresses, Rei becomes something unique, just like the lone, half-demolished building. Other things: a) Why are these buildings there anyway, and why are they abandoned? I believe they were constructed 'cause ppl were homeless and poor after SI, and this kind of buildings are cheap to buy/live in. After the economical situation improved, everyone moved away. b) Did you notice that Rei buys a table over the course of the movie? Of course, the eating motif (as something ppl to together, something enjoyable in the realm of the living - the latest CR translations indicate that "eating" is an important motif in Rebuild, inspired by Anno's wife.) Also, the "filling" of an empty room, just as Rei's empty heart is filled - the lunch box, the table, and the SDAT player, which will probably also end up there. One may or may not see the glasses as the first step in this row. c) Why was Rei's place left intact/why wasn't she asked to move out? Remember, it is implied that she "chose" the place's style to replicate her room - It looks like a lab, and lab is "cozy familiar home" to Rei. Well, not cozy, but something vaguely like that. It's possible that Gendo intervened and made sure his little girl's house stays where it is. Your two cents on this, dudes?

[]: Re: Rei's Appartment             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed Jul 06, 2011 6:27 pm Kendrix wrote: a) Why are these buildings there anyway, and why are they abandoned?

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Wed Jul 06, 2011 6:42 pm Although, Rei might be an expendable, the public doesn't know that (well, depending what you mean by expendable). Unless, if you mean that NERV personnel ask her to leave, in which its isn't likely that they really care.

[]:             Quote Post by closet nerd » Thu Jul 07, 2011 10:23 am This inconsistency is forgivable frankly I didn't even notice it :P

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Jul 07, 2011 11:58 am I meant why don't the authorities demand she move out because they want to build something else there? As I said, Gendo probably stepped in.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Thu Jul 07, 2011 3:13 pm The Magi run the city. They wouldn't want to ask Rei to move out.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Thu Jul 07, 2011 7:45 pm JoeD80 wrote: The Magi run the city. They wouldn't want to ask Rei to move out.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Fri Jul 08, 2011 6:03 am KnightmareX13 wrote: makes sense, the magi are made up of different elements of Naoko's personality, and at least one part of her personality hates Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Jul 08, 2011 6:13 am Jornophelanthas wrote: We have no indication Naoko even exists in this continuity
